A formal test of knowledge or proficiency.
example: She studied hard for her final exam.

meaning: A short, informal test.
example: We have a vocabulary quiz every Friday.

meaning: The evaluation of the nature, quality, or ability of someone or something.
example: The teacher used the assessment to determine our reading level.

meaning: An exam given in the middle of an academic term.
example: I need to review my notes before the midterm.

meaning: An examination at the end of a term or academic year.
example: The final counts for fifty percent of our grade.

meaning: A quiz given without any prior warning.
example: I hate it when the professor springs a pop quiz on us.

meaning: A test in which the candidate answers questions aloud.
example: For her Spanish class, she had to take an oral exam.

meaning: An examination conducted in writing.
example: You must pass a written test before getting a driver's license.

meaning: Offering a choice of several answers, of which only one is correct.
example: The history exam was mostly multiple-choice questions.

meaning: An examination in which students must demonstrate practical skills.
example: The chemistry practical involved conducting an experiment.

meaning: A test that is administered and scored in a consistent manner.
example: Many universities require standardized test scores for admission.

meaning: A test designed to identify a student's strengths and weaknesses.
example: The diagnostic test helped the tutor create a customized study plan.

meaning: A test given to students entering an institution to determine knowledge or proficiency.
example: He took a placement test to see which math class he should enroll in.

meaning: An examination taken for practice before an official examination.
example: Taking a mock exam helps reduce test anxiety.

meaning: An essay or dissertation written by a student.
example: She has a ten-page paper due on Monday.

meaning: A speech or talk in which a new product, idea, or piece of work is shown and explained.
example: His group gave a presentation on climate change.

meaning: An individual or collaborative enterprise that is carefully planned to achieve a particular aim.
example: The science project took three weeks to complete.

meaning: The making of a judgment about the amount, number, or value of something.
example: The teacher's evaluation highlighted his strong writing skills.

meaning: A formal test of a person's knowledge or proficiency in a particular subject or skill.
example: The university entrance examination was extremely difficult.

meaning: A long essay or dissertation involving personal research, written for a university degree.
example: She is writing her master's thesis on medieval history.

meaning: A long essay on a particular subject, especially one written as a requirement for the Doctor of Philosophy degree.
example: Defending a dissertation is the final step in a PhD program.

meaning: An examination that students must pass to be admitted to an educational institution.
example: He spent months preparing for the medical school entrance exam.

meaning: A standardized examination given to candidates for certification in a profession.
example: The nursing student passed her board exam on the first try.

meaning: An exam that measures a person's level of skill or knowledge.
example: She took an English proficiency test to study abroad.

meaning: A test designed to determine a person's ability in a particular skill or field of knowledge.
example: The career counselor administered an aptitude test.

meaning: The evaluation of a student's progress throughout a course, rather than by a single examination.
example: This course relies on continuous assessment rather than a final exam.

meaning: A mark indicating the quality of a student's work.
example: She received a high grade on her essay.

meaning: A point or score given for academic work.
example: The pass mark for this test is 60 percent.

meaning: The number of points achieved in a test or examination.
example: He got a perfect score on the math quiz.

meaning: To be successful in an examination or test.
example: I hope to pass all my classes this semester.

meaning: To be unsuccessful in an examination or test.
example: If you don't study, you might fail the course.

meaning: A high grade awarded for outstanding academic achievement.
example: She graduated with distinction from her university.

meaning: A grade signifying a level of achievement above average but below distinction.
example: He passed his piano exam with merit.

meaning: An acknowledgment of having completed a course of study.
example: You need 120 credits to graduate.

meaning: Grade Point Average; a number representing the average value of the accumulated final grades.
example: Maintaining a high GPA is important for scholarships.

meaning: A measure used in statistics indicating the value below which a given percentage of observations fall.
example: His test score was in the 99th percentile.

meaning: A position in a hierarchy based on academic performance.
example: She holds the number one rank in her graduating class.

meaning: To form an idea of the amount, number, or value of; assess.
example: The teacher will evaluate our speaking skills next week.

meaning: To evaluate or estimate the nature, ability, or quality of.
example: Exams are used to assess what students have learned.

meaning: A scoring guide used to evaluate the quality of students' constructed responses.
example: The professor provided a rubric for the term paper.

meaning: A unit of credit or scoring in an examination.
example: Each multiple-choice question is worth one point.

meaning: Optional academic work that allows a student to earn additional points.
example: Doing the optional presentation will give you extra credit.

meaning: A deduction of points for an error or rule violation.
example: There is a penalty for handing in assignments late.

meaning: A grading system where grades are assigned based on the relative performance of students.
example: The professor graded the notoriously difficult exam on a curve.

meaning: An official record of a student's work, showing courses taken and grades achieved.
example: I need to request my official transcript for my job application.

meaning: A teacher's written assessment of a student's work, progress, and conduct, sent home to parents.
example: Her parents were proud of her excellent report card.

meaning: A standard or level that is considered typical or usual.
example: My math grade is above average this semester.

meaning: A student, typically having the highest academic achievements, who delivers the valedictory at graduation.
example: She worked incredibly hard to become the high school valedictorian.

meaning: With academic honors (in university degrees).
example: He graduated cum laude with a degree in engineering.

meaning: A class or course of degree studies more specialized than an ordinary pass.
example: She took an honors class in literature.

meaning: A graduated system used to assess or score something.
example: The test is graded on a scale of one to ten.

meaning: A piece of furniture with a flat or sloping surface for writing or reading.
example: Please clear your desk before the test begins.

meaning: A computer that is portable and suitable for use while traveling.
example: Many students take notes on their laptops during lectures.

meaning: A small, flat computer that is controlled by touching the screen.
example: The school provided tablets for digital textbooks.

meaning: A book used as a standard work for the study of a particular subject.
example: Please open your history textbook to page forty.

meaning: A small book with blank or ruled pages for writing notes in.
example: I bought a new notebook for my math class.

meaning: A student's book containing instruction and exercises relating to a particular subject.
example: Complete the exercises in the grammar workbook.

meaning: An instrument for writing or drawing with ink.
example: You must use a blue or black pen for the exam.

meaning: An instrument for writing or drawing, consisting of a thin stick of graphite.
example: Make sure you have a sharpened pencil for the scantron test.

meaning: A straight strip or cylinder used to draw straight lines or measure distances.
example: Use a ruler to draw the margins straight.

meaning: An instrument for measuring angles, typically in the form of a flat semicircle marked with degrees.
example: We used a protractor in geometry class today.

meaning: An instrument for drawing circles and arcs.
example: Use your compass to draw a perfect circle.

meaning: A small electronic device used for performing mathematical calculations.
example: Calculators are not permitted during the basic arithmetic test.

meaning: A diagrammatic representation of an area of land or sea.
example: There is a large world map on the classroom wall.

meaning: A spherical representation of the earth.
example: The geography teacher spun the globe to find Australia.

meaning: A board for displaying notices or announcements.
example: Check the bulletin board for the club meeting schedule.

meaning: A sheet of information in the form of a table, graph, or diagram.
example: The periodic table chart hangs in the chemistry lab.

meaning: A long, thin rod used to direct attention to a particular location, especially on a map or chart.
example: The teacher used a laser pointer during his presentation.

meaning: A book or electronic resource that lists the words of a language and gives their meaning.
example: Look up any words you don't know in the dictionary.

meaning: A book that lists words in groups of synonyms and related concepts.
example: A thesaurus is very helpful when writing an essay.

meaning: A device for fastening together sheets of paper with a staple.
example: Can I borrow your stapler for my research paper?

meaning: A device used to make holes in paper.
example: Use the hole punch so you can put these handouts in your binder.

meaning: An instrument used for cutting cloth, paper, and other thin material.
example: We need scissors and glue for this art project.

meaning: An adhesive substance used for sticking objects or materials together.
example: Make sure you put the cap back on the glue.

meaning: A narrow strip of material, typically used to hold or fasten something.
example: I need some tape to fix my torn paper.

meaning: A broad felt-tipped pen used to overlay transparent fluorescent color on text.
example: Use a highlighter to mark important concepts in your book.

meaning: A cover for holding loose sheets of paper.
example: Keep all your syllabus and handouts in your binder.

meaning: A folding cover or holder, typically made of stiff paper or plastic, for storing loose papers.
example: Put your completed homework in the green folder.

meaning: A bag with shoulder straps that allow it to be carried on one's back.
example: His backpack was heavy with textbooks.

meaning: A small lockable closet or compartment, typically as one of a number placed together for public use.
example: Store your gym clothes in your locker.

meaning: To reread work done previously to improve one's knowledge of a subject.
example: I need to revise my notes for the upcoming test.

meaning: To commit to memory; learn by heart.
example: Actors have to memorize a lot of lines.

meaning: To devote time and attention to acquiring knowledge on a subject.
example: She likes to study in the quiet library.

meaning: To study intensively over a short period of time just before an examination.
example: It's not effective to cram the night before a final exam.

meaning: To gain or acquire knowledge of or skill in something by study, experience, or being taught.
example: Children learn foreign languages very quickly.

meaning: To look at and comprehend the meaning of written or printed matter.
example: Please read the first chapter for homework.

meaning: To mark letters, words, or other symbols on a surface, typically paper, with a pen, pencil, or similar implement.
example: You need to write a 500-word essay.

meaning: To write down details from a lecture or text in order to remember them.
example: Make sure you take notes during the presentation.

meaning: To draw attention to or emphasize, often by using a brightly colored pen on text.
example: Highlight the most important vocabulary words in the text.

meaning: To draw a line under a word or phrase to give emphasis or indicate special type.
example: Underline the thesis statement in the introductory paragraph.

meaning: To prepare a preliminary version of a document.
example: I will draft the outline tonight and write the essay tomorrow.

meaning: To prepare written material for publication by correcting, condensing, or otherwise modifying it.
example: You should always edit your work before submitting it.

meaning: To read printer's proofs or other written or printed material and mark any errors.
example: Please proofread this document for spelling mistakes.

meaning: To produce an idea or way of solving a problem by holding a spontaneous group discussion.
example: Let's brainstorm some ideas for our group project.

meaning: To give a brief statement of the main points of something.
example: Can you summarize the plot of the novel?

meaning: To give a summary of something.
example: Outline your argument before you start writing.

meaning: To examine or assess something formally with the possibility or intention of instituting change if necessary.
example: We will review the homework answers at the beginning of class.

meaning: To perform an activity or exercise a skill repeatedly or regularly in order to improve or maintain one's proficiency.
example: You must practice playing the piano every day.

meaning: To practice a play, piece of music, or other work for later public performance.
example: The students stayed after school to rehearse the play.

meaning: To pay particular attention to.
example: It is hard to focus when there is so much noise.

meaning: To focus all one's attention on a particular object or activity.
example: I can't concentrate on my reading with the television on.

meaning: To examine methodically and in detail the constitution or structure of something.
example: We had to analyze a poem in literature class.

meaning: To explain the meaning of information, words, or actions.
example: How do you interpret the author's message?

meaning: To investigate systematically.
example: She is researching the effects of climate change.

meaning: To carry out a systematic or formal inquiry to discover and examine the facts of an incident, allegation, etc.
example: The science students will investigate chemical reactions.

meaning: To quote a passage, book, or author as evidence for or justification of an argument or statement.
example: Make sure you cite your sources in the bibliography.

meaning: To provide a book or article with citations of sources of information.
example: You must reference every author you quote.

meaning: To repeat or copy out a group of words from a text or speech.
example: He decided to quote Shakespeare in his essay.

meaning: To express the meaning of something written or spoken using different words, especially to achieve greater clarity.
example: Instead of quoting directly, try to paraphrase the paragraph.

meaning: To take the work or an idea of someone else and pass it off as one's own.
example: Students who plagiarize will receive a failing grade.

meaning: To act dishonestly or unfairly in order to gain an advantage, especially in a game or examination.
example: He was caught trying to cheat on the math final.

meaning: To make a similar or identical version of; reproduce.
example: Do not copy another student's homework.

meaning: To work jointly on an activity, especially to produce or create something.
example: The teacher asked us to collaborate on the group project.

meaning: To take part in an action or endeavor.
example: You need to participate more in class discussions.

meaning: To be present at an event, meeting, or function.
example: It is mandatory to attend all lectures.

meaning: To fail to attend or participate in.
example: If you skip class, you will miss important information.

meaning: To officially register as a member of an institution or a student on a course.
example: I plan to enroll in the advanced French class.

Middle Eastern dish of spiced mashed chickpeas formed into balls and deep-fried.
South American dish of marinated raw fish or seafood.
French vegetable dish of onions, zucchini, tomatoes, eggplant, and peppers.
Italian dish of small pasta envelopes containing filling.
Chinese duck dish known for its thin, crisp skin.
A hot drink made from roasted and ground coffee beans.
A hot drink made by infusing tea leaves in boiling water.

Strong black coffee made by forcing steam through ground coffee beans.
Coffee made with milk that has been frothed up with steam.
Coffee made with espresso and hot steamed milk.

Espresso coffee diluted with hot water.

Tea made by boiling tea leaves with milk, sugar, and spices.
An infusion made from herbs or spices in hot water.
Red wine heated with various mulling spices.
Heated apple cider, often spiced.
Savory liquid made by simmering bones, meat, or vegetables.
Clear, colorless liquid essential for life.

A drink made from lemon juice, water, and sugar.
Chilled tea served with ice.
Chilled coffee beverage.
Thick drink of fresh fruit pureed with milk or yogurt.

Alcoholic drink made from fermented malt flavored with hops.
Alcoholic drink made from fermented grape juice.
Mixed alcoholic drink.

Beverage designed to replace water and electrolytes.
